checks.reason: The source-checked node is anchored to Cambridge's 1155-64 dating for Giovanni Scriba's oldest surviving European notarial cartularies.
The registers authenticated commercial agreements and legal acts.
The anchor Giovanni Scriba register is described by the State Archive of Genoa as a paper manuscript.
The scoped medieval Italian register tradition belongs to Roman-law-influenced legal systems.
The broad charterparty placeholder is dated before the source-backed 1155 notarial-register node.
Craft marks, hallmarks, seals, and inspections should not directly depend on notarial registers without a narrower notarized-certification source.
The 1086 civic-record scope should not depend on a later Genoese notarial-register tradition.
